revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 pixel-art terrain tileset sheet. Strict 4x4 grid, 16 frames, each frame exactly 16x16 px. Do not draw grid lines, borders between cells, labels, text, UI, watermark, or tile IDs. All art must stay inside its own 16x16 cell and keep identical scale. Crisp SNES-style 16-bit pixel art, hard pixel edges, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, one-pixel dark outline only where useful, top-left light source.

Subject: top-down flat dressed cut-stone coping border tiles edging a raised matcha-grass garden in a bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette. Pale light grey-green dressed rectangular capstones, faint warm-green tinge, soft warm sunlight. The coping is a single neat low flat band along the grass edge, viewed almost straight down from above with only a very slight forward tilt. Not isometric, not a tall wall. Everything that is not the stone band is plain deep matcha grass. Stone texture should look like neatly dressed kasaishi cap stones: rectangular pale grey tops, subtle warm-green speckles, tiny moss/lichen only on specified variants.

Layout and tile semantics, reading left to right, top to bottom:
Row 1: cell0 north edge straight with horizontal stone band along the top outer edge and grass below; cell1 north edge straight variation with subtle stone breakup; cell2 west edge straight with vertical stone band on the left side and grass to the right; cell3 east edge straight with vertical stone band on the right side and grass to the left.
Row 2 outer convex corners where the stone band turns: cell4 NW outer corner; cell5 NE outer corner; cell6 SW outer corner; cell7 SE outer corner.
Row 3 inner concave corners cut into the grass border: cell8 NW inner concave corner; cell9 NE inner concave corner; cell10 SW inner concave corner; cell11 SE inner concave corner.
Row 4 mossy straight-edge variants: cell12 north mossy; cell13 west mossy; cell14 east mossy; cell15 north with one small lichen spot.

Tilemap quality: each tile must fill edge to edge and be tileable. Avoid repeated square stamp look by varying tiny grass pixels, edge breakup, and stone highlights while keeping the set cohesive. Keep transitions natural, with the stone band blending cleanly into the grass, not forming hard rectangular blocks beyond the intended coping band. Use broad matcha grass with low-frequency texture, not rows of identical blades.

Strict exclusions: no posts, pillars, lanterns, gateposts, bollards, gears, wheels, arches, openings, dark holes, statues, ornaments, buildings, objects, characters, animals, creatures, logos, letter emblems, weapons, armor, modern items, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, text, grid lines, or isometric distortion. Original generic fantasy terrain only; no copyrighted character likenesses or logos. If any area would otherwise be empty outside cells, fill with solid #FF00FF magenta, but the 16 tiles themselves should be fully filled by grass and stone.
